message:
chore(beads): sync issue updated_at timestamps for in-progress reality-check sweep

Picks up the latest .beads/issues.jsonl state for six in-flight beads
that were touched by parallel agents during the current
ConnectorSuite/gossip work. The change is purely metadata churn
(updated_at bumps on already-existing issues) and contains no
status, priority, dependency, comment, or close-reason changes.

Issues updated (id — title):
- flywheel_connectors-39jl7 — [CONFORMANCE-GAP] gossip: missing
  coverage for RevocationPush rate-limit
  (priority_gossip_interval_ms, max_revocation_push_peers).
  Touched by the same agent group landing the gossip
  Request/Response dispatch work.
- flywheel_connectors-4f7ic — [REALITY-CHECK][MEDIUM]
  connectors/dingtalk: missing tests/ ConnectorSuite-based
  happy-path test.
- flywheel_connectors-4gysg — [REALITY-CHECK][MEDIUM]
  connectors/supabase: tests/ lacks ConnectorSuite-based
  happy-path coverage.
- flywheel_connectors-5fp46 — [REALITY-CHECK][MEDIUM]
  connectors/gcp: tests/ lacks ConnectorSuite-based happy-path
  coverage. Direct counterpart to the new
  connectors/gcp/tests/connector_suite_happy_path.rs landed in
  the previous commit on this branch.
- flywheel_connectors-jtjo4 — [REALITY-CHECK][MEDIUM]
  connectors/twitter: tests/ lacks ConnectorSuite-based
  happy-path coverage.
- flywheel_connectors-u2ey1 — [REALITY-CHECK][MEDIUM]
  connectors/synology-chat: tests/ lacks ConnectorSuite-based
  happy-path coverage.

Why this is a separate commit:
- Keeps the bead metadata sync independent of the connector test
  and mesh node dispatch changes so a future archaeologist can
  diff each layer cleanly.
- Mirrors the project pattern of small `chore(beads): ...`
  commits for routine .beads/issues.jsonl drift produced by the
  beads CLI / multi-agent activity.

Verified before commit:
- git diff -U0 shows no inserted/deleted JSON lines beyond
  updated_at timestamp swaps.
- No "close_reason", "deleted", "status":"closed" transitions
  in the diff.
